summary: Muffled audience recording captures a strong second‑half comeback highlighted by Iko‑Iko, Way to Go Home and an adventurous Space.
review: The show starts solidly with “Stranger” but loses steam until the intermission, after which the band revives the energy with a rolling Iko‑Iko, Vince’s on‑point keyboards on “Way to Go Home,” and a far‑out extended Space. Recording quality is only so‑so – a muffled metallic sheen on vocals and a ghostly stadium echo give it a lo‑fi feel, but the performance shines through.

ai_rating.rationale: Reviewers rate the show 4–5 stars for its musical resurgence despite noting only average (so‑so) sound quality, leading to a weighted rating that reflects strong performance but modest recording.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Muffled metallic sheen on vocals, ghostly stadium echo, overall so‑so clarity.

show_quality.setlist_flow: First half felt plodding despite a strong start, but the second half after intermission surged with energy and memorable jams.

band_member_comments.Bob: Bob Weir’s raspy vocal tone is noted during the performance.
band_member_comments.Keys: Vince’s keyboard work on “Way to Go Home” is praised, including his in‑tune singing.
